data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Minimizes the number of choices to streamline decision-making and reduce user cognitive load.
data.technical_spec: Interfaces are simplified, avoiding clutter and focusing on core functionality.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Designs with easy-to-access controls by placing important interactions within quick reach.
data.technical_spec: Touch targets are large and positioned for intuitive use on various devices.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Balances aesthetic design with usability, ensuring visually appealing and functional interfaces.
data.technical_spec: Invests in high-quality imagery and consistent branding elements.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Groups related items together to create intuitive and organized layouts.
data.technical_spec: Grouping and spacing are used to imply relationships between content.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Conveys information in manageable chunks to facilitate retention and comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Content is subdivided into logical sections with clear headings.
